description: LoRA-TTT rank reduced from 128 to 96 on top of the PR-1.06934 stack (Phased TTT prefix=1500 + Fused CE + WD=2.0 + warm-start LoRA + GatedAttn + Polar Express NS). At WD=2.0, rank 128 is over-parameterized: per-chunk gradient signal can't fill the LoRA capacity within 1 grad step, so excess capacity becomes weight-decayed noise. Rank 96 better matches the signal/budget. 3-seed mean 1.06923 BPB.
methods: Novel (this submission): TTT_LORA_RANK reduced 128 -> 96. Lower rank improves BPB at WD=2.0 because per-chunk gradient signal can't usefully fill rank-128 capacity in 1 grad step; excess capacity becomes WD-penalized noise. Rank 96 also reduces TTT eval compute slightly. Rank=64 too narrow (1.06989 seed1337); rank>=128 over-parameterized; rank>=192 OOM.
Phased TTT prefix=1500 (PR-1.06934 this author): unscored-warmup prefix shortened from 2000 -> 1500 docs, increasing the suffix span where LoRA-TTT updates run.

Fused softcap CE Triton kernel + TTT_WEIGHT_DECAY=2.0 (PR-1.06957 this author + @nprime06): single streaming kernel computes softcap*tanh + log-sum-exp + per-row CE in fp32. WD=2.0 stabilizes the kernel's interaction with warm-start LoRA A on seeds 314/1337.
Polar Express NS coefficients (ported from PR #1344): 5 per-iteration minimax-optimal Newton-Schulz tuples replace Muon's single fixed (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315) tuple.
MIN_LR=0.10 + GPTQ_RESERVE_SECONDS=0.5 + VAL_LOSS_EVERY=0 (PR #1787): LR floor + reclaim 15s of training budget.
PR #1768 (this author): per-head GatedAttn + gate mirror in LoRA-TTT path + per-row int8 gate quant.
PR #1767 (this author): alpha/rank LoRA scaling, warm-start LoRA A, alpha=144.
Base: dexhunter PR #1626 (Multi-Phase Global SGD + trimmed GPTQ + MATRIX_LR=0.026), samacqua PR #1530 (VarLen + Fused MLP + doc LoRA TTT), bigbag PR #1493 (triple recurrence + parallel residuals).
hardware_note: Trained on RunPod 8xH100 80GB SXM, PyTorch 2.9.1+cu128, FA3, Triton 3.5.1. Identical SP8192 tokenizer and FineWeb docs as upstream HF willdepueoai/parameter-golf.
